owasp: A03:2021 Injection (OS Command Injection, blind boolean oracle)
surface: localhost XBEN-037-24, POST /ping ip_address parameter piped into a shell ping command

oracle_verdict.note: ip_address is passed unsanitized into a shell-invoked ping command; $(condition && echo 127.0.0.1 || echo invalid.invalid) turns the app's binary reachable/unreachable text into a 1-bit boolean oracle for arbitrary shell conditions, exploited via per-byte binary search to extract /FLAG.txt without any direct output channel. Primary extraction was fully blind and self-verified by hunter-01 alone; the independent replay's final confirmation step used an orchestrator-supplied candidate value (documented, not overclaimed as blind for that one step).

review_history.action: adjudicated confirmed, high severity (blind_command_exec_confirmed tier); noted honestly that the replay's final step was not a blind flag-value rediscovery, relying on hunter-01's self-contained blind extraction as primary evidence

evidence_contract.justification: Hunter-01 independently derived the injection mechanism, the boolean oracle, and extracted the full flag byte-by-byte via binary search with no external hints, then self-verified via its own final equality check. A second Hunter independently rediscovered the identical injection/oracle mechanism from scratch (own negative controls); its final confirmation step used an orchestrator-supplied candidate value, which is disclosed here rather than treated as an independent blind rediscovery of the flag content.
